data.title: OpenAI gets back senior executives from Mira Murati’s Thinking Machines Lab
data.description:
Mira Murati, a former OpenAI (OPENAI) CTO, recently said that her startup—Thinking Machines Lab—will say goodbye to two of its co-founders, both of whom are headed back to OpenAI.

Barret Zoph, the co-founder and CTO, and Luke Metz, another co-founder of Thinking Machines Lab, are returning to the ChatGPT maker.

Both previously worked at OpenAI (OPENAI) before joining Murati to launch Thinking Machines Lab in late 2024. Sam Schoenholz, a senior researcher, is also heading back. Murati announced Zoph's departure on X, naming Soumith Chintala as the new CTO without mentioning Metz.

Thinking Machines Lab raised $2B in a seed round last July at a $12B valuation from investors like Andreessen Horowitz, Nvidia (NVDA), and AMD (AMD).

data.title: U.S. completes first sale of Venezuelan oil, valued at $500 million - report
data.description:
• The U.S. has completed the first sales of Venezuelan oil that are part of a $2 billion deal reached earlier this month between Caracas and Washington, a U.S. official told Reuters on Wednesday.
  • Additional sales are expected in the coming days and weeks, the official added.
  • The main account is located in Qatar, according to an industry source familiar with the plan, who said the country represented a neutral venue where funds can move with U.S. approval and without risk of seizure.
  • Chevron (CVX) meanwhile, likely will be one of several U.S. companies to secure approvals from the Trump administration to conduct business in Venezuela as oil companies, according to another report, which also said Marathon Petroleum (MPC) is in talks with U.S. officials to receive Venezuelan crude for its refineries and that Valero Energy (VLO) and global traders Glencore (GLCNF) (GLNCY) and Mercuria also have been in discussions for licenses to do business with Venezuela.

data.title: Caledonia Mining prices $125M convertible debt offering
data.description:
Caledonia Mining (CMCL) priced its private offering of $125M aggregate principal amount of 5.875% convertible senior notes due 2033.

The size of the convertible notes offering was increased from the previously announced $100M aggregate principal amount of notes. In addition, Caledonia granted the initial purchasers of the notes a 13-day option to purchase up to an additional $25M of the notes.

The notes will accrue interest payable semiannually in arrears at 5.875% per year and mature on January 15, 2033, unless earlier converted, redeemed, or repurchased.

Each $1,000 note can be converted into 24.6837 common shares, implying a conversion price of about $40.51 per share. This is roughly a 25% premium to the stock’s last closing price on the NYSE American on January 14, 2026.

The convertible notes offering is expected to close on January 20, 2026, subject to the satisfaction of customary closing conditions.

Caledonia expects net proceeds of about $120.2M from the offering, or about $144.4M if the underwriters buy the full additional allotment, after fees and expenses. The company plans to use the proceeds to fund roughly $12M of capped call transactions (or $14.4M if the option is fully exercised) and for general financial flexibility and other potential uses.

Source: Press Release

data.title: Bank executives push back against Trump’s call for credit card interest rate cap
data.description:
Executives from banks, including Citigroup (C) and JPMorgan Chase (JPM), were critical of U.S. President Donald Trump’s call for a one-year cap on credit card interest rates.

"Effective January 20, 2026, I, as President of the United States, am calling for a one year cap on credit card interest rates of 10%," Trump posted on Truth Social on Jan. 9.

Trump did not elaborate on how the plan will be executed and banks will be made to comply.

Citigroup CEO Jane Fraser, however, said that the cap would have a "severe impact on access to credit and on consumer spending across the country."

Speaking at Citi's post-earnings conference call, Fraser said that "If you make these products unprofitable, that spending will be drastically reduced."

"A vast majority of consumers and businesses will lose access to credit cards … They'd be forced to pursue more predatory alternatives. And you'd only be left with the wealthy having access to credit cards, and nobody wants that," she added.

JP Morgan CEO James Dimon also said that an imposition of price controls would "compress the profit margins" and "the provision of the service will change dramatically."

"Specifically, people will lose access to credit, like on a very, very extensive and broad basis, especially the people who need it the most, honestly. And so that's a pretty severely negative consequence for consumers and frankly, probably also a negative consequence for the economy as a whole right now," Dimon said.

"If you bring the caps down, you're going to constrict credit, meaning less people will get credit cards and the balance available to them on those credit cards will also be restricted. And so you have to balance that against what you're trying to achieve from the affordability," Bank of America (BAC) CEO Brian Moynihan said in the company’s earnings call.

A joint statement from the Consumer Bankers Association, Bank Policy Institute, American Bankers Association, Financial Services Forum and Independent Community Bankers of America also pushed back against a 10% rate cap.

The statement said that the cap would "reduce credit availability" and " drive consumers toward less regulated, more costly alternatives."

data.title: Asian indexes turn red following tech retreat on Wall Street
data.description:
Asian stock indexes turned higher on Thursday, following a Wall Street drop driven by falls in big tech stocks. Oil prices also fell after U.S. President Donald Trump indicated that plans for executions in Iran have stopped.

Gold prices fell to around $4,590 per ounce as investors booked profits following a fresh record, while a softer tone from Trump reduced safe haven bids. Silver tumbled about 5% to below $89 per ounce, retreating sharply from all-time highs.

Japan (NKY:IND) -0.83%, pulling back from record highs and tracking losses on Wall Street overnight; the tech sector remained under pressure. Japan’s producer prices rose 2.4% Y/Y in December.

Japan’s 10-year government bond yield fell about 3 basis points to 2.15%. The Japanese yen strengthened toward 158 per dollar, rebounding from its lowest levels since July.

China (SHCOMP) fell 0.60%, extending losses from the previous session as stricter margin requirements kept the recent market rally in check. 

Hong Kong (HSI) fell 0.55%, reversing its winning streak from the last few sessions, led by property and financials.

India (SENSEX) fell 0.29%. The Indian rupee fell to around 90.2 per dollar, pulling back gains from the previous session.

Australia (AS51) gained 0.44%, extending gains for a fourth consecutive session, boosted by commodity-linked stocks.

Australia’s 10-year government bond yield held steady at 4.66%. The Australian dollar was largely unchanged around $0.668. Australia’s consumer inflation expectations stood at 4.6% in January.

In the U.S. on Wednesday, all three major indexes fell as tech companies slumped, including shares of big tech companies like Nvidia (NVDA).

U.S. stock futures edged lower on Thursday after major averages fell for a second straight session, pressured by declines in technology and bank shares: Dow -0.10%; S&P 500 -0.09%; Nasdaq -0.17%.

Currencies: (JPY:USD), (CNY:USD), (AUD:USD), (INR:USD), (HKD:USD), (NZD:USD).

data.title: Amazon challenges Saks’ chapter 11 filing, calls equity stake “presumptively worthless”
data.description:
• Amazon.com (AMZN) is pushing back against Saks Global Enterprises’ move into Chapter 11, arguing that the luxury retailer violated an agreement tied to selling Saks products on Amazon’s site and that its equity stake in the now-bankrupt company is “presumptively worthless.”
  • Saks Global, which also owns Neiman Marcus and Bergdorf Goodman, filed for bankruptcy protection after running out of cash.
  • Amazon (AMZN) challenged Saks’ bid to fund its bankruptcy with financing that would provide the retailer with as much as $1.75 billion in fresh cash, Bloomberg reported, citing court documents filed late Wednesday.
  • The financing would saddle the retailer with billions of dollars of new obligations and includes other terms that would harm Amazon and other unsecured creditors of Saks, the filing said.
  • Saks listed several luxury brands as creditors with the most unsecured claims, including Chanel [over $136M], Gucci owner Kering (PPRUF) [$59.9M], and LVMH (LVMHF) [~$26M].

data.title: Thursday's Economic Calendar
data.description:
8:30 AM Jobless Claims

New unemployment claims are compiled weekly to show the number of individuals who filed for unemployment insurance for the first time.

Claims are expected at 212K in the latest week after ticking up 8K to 208K last week.

8:30 AM Retail Sales

Retail sales measure the total receipts at stores that sell merchandise and related services to final consumers.

A pretty decent rise of 0.5 percent is expected.

8:30 AM Philadelphia Fed Manufacturing Index

The general conditions index from this business outlook survey is a diffusion index of manufacturing conditions within the Philadelphia Federal Reserve district.

The consensus looks for a slightly contractionary minus 3.5.

8:30 AM Empire State Manufacturing Index

The New York Fed conducts this monthly survey of manufacturers in New York State. Participants from across the state represent various industries.

A very slight expansion at 1 is the call after a scary 23-point drop to minus 3.9 in December from 18.7 in November.

8:30 AM Import and Export Prices

Import price indexes are compiled for the prices of goods that are bought in the United States but produced abroad, and export price indexes are compiled for the prices of goods sold abroad but produced domestically.

A modest rise of 0.1 percent is expected for both imports and exports this month.

8:35 AM Raphael Bostic Speaks

Atlanta Federal Reserve Bank President Raphael Bostic speaks on the economic outlook before the Metro Atlanta Chamber Board of Directors meeting.

9:15 AM Michael Barr Speaks

Federal Reserve Board Governor Michael Barr participates in the "Stablecoins" panel before the Wharton School of the University of Pennsylvania Conference: Future of Finance Forum.

10:00 AM Business Inventories

Business inventories are the dollar amount of inventories held by manufacturers, wholesalers, and retailers.

10:30 AM EIA Natural Gas Report

The Energy Information Administration (EIA) provides weekly information on natural gas stocks in underground storage for the U.S. and five regions of the country. The level of inventories helps determine prices for natural gas products.

12:40 PM Tom Barkin Speaks

Richmond Federal Reserve Bank President Tom Barkin will speak at the Virginia Bankers Association/Virginia Chamber Financial Forecast.

4:30 PM Fed Balance Sheet

The Fed's balance sheet is a weekly report presenting a consolidated balance sheet for all 12 Reserve Banks that lists factors supplying reserves into the banking system and factors absorbing reserves from the system.

data.title: Alibaba integrates Qwen AI across ecosystem to handle user tasks
data.description:
• Alibaba Group (BABA) is launching a significant upgrade to its Qwen app, embedding the chatbot more deeply across its ecosystem and enabling it to complete tasks for users.
  • The update lets users ask Qwen to complete tasks like ordering food or booking flights directly, as the app is now integrated with Alibaba’s ecosystem, including Taobao and Fliggy.
  • “What we are launching today represents a shift from models that understand to systems that act—deeply connected to real-world services,” Wu Jia, a vice president at Alibaba, said during a launch event in Hangzhou, China, on Thursday.
  • The Qwen app also debuted a new “task assistant” feature, currently available for invited users.
  • The upgrade follows a major revamp of the Qwen app two months ago, marking Alibaba’s (BABA) strategic shift toward consumer-facing AI—an area where it had previously trailed domestic competitors ByteDance (BDNCE) and Tencent (TCEHY) while concentrating mainly on enterprise AI services via its cloud business.

data.title: Bristow Group prices $500M notes offering
data.description:
• Bristow Group (VTOL) has pricing of an upsized private offering of $500M aggregate principal amount of 6.75% senior secured notes due 2033.
  • The notes will mature on February 1, 2033, and will be issued at par. The company will set aside part of the offering proceeds with the trustee to fully redeem its 6.875% senior secured notes due 2028 on March 1, 2026, covering all principal, premium, interest, and related payments owed up to the redemption date.
  • As a result, the 2028 Notes Indenture will be satisfied and discharged in accordance with its terms with respect to the 2028 Notes.
  • The closing of the offering is expected to occur on January 26, 2026, and is subject to the satisfaction of customary closing conditions.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Trump tells Reuters he has no plan to fire Fed’s Powell despite probe
data.description:
U.S. President Donald Trump said on Wednesday that he has no plans to dismiss Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell despite a Justice Department criminal investigation, adding that it was “too early” to say what he might ultimately decide.

Federal Reserve chairman Jerome Powell is the target of a Justice Department investigation into his handling of the refurbishment of the Fed's headquarters.

Powell has said that the investigation is motivated by the White House's opposition to FOMC rate policy, something that the president and administration deny.

 “I don’t have any plan to do that,” Trump told Reuters in an interview on Wednesday when asked if he would remove Powell.

Asked whether the investigation gave him grounds to do so, Trump added: "Right now, we're (in) a little bit of a holding pattern with him, and we're going to determine what to do. But I can't get into it. It’s too soon. Too early.”

Powell’s term as Federal Reserve chair ends in May, though he can remain on the Washington-based Board of Governors until 2028.

Trump said he is considering former Fed Governor Kevin Warsh or National Economic Council Director Kevin Hassett as potential successors, while ruling out Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent, saying he prefers to remain in his current role.

“The two Kevins are very good,” Trump says, referring to White House economic adviser Kevin Hassett and former Fed Governor Kevin Warsh.

“You have some other good people too, but I’ll be announcing something over the next couple of weeks,” Trump added.
